parts.answer: OUTPUT "Option Students Staff Total" OUTPUT "A ", TotalAS, TotalAF, (TotalAS + TotalAF) OUTPUT "B ", TotalBS, TotalBF, (TotalBS + TotalBF) OUTPUT "C ", TotalCS, TotalCF, (TotalCS + TotalCF) OUTPUT "D ", TotalDS, TotalDF, (TotalDS + TotalDF) OUTPUT "E ", TotalES, TotalEF, (TotalES + TotalEF) two from: MP1 Change the maximum preferences constant to 6 MP2 Change the input prompt to show six options MP3 Change the loop counter MP4 Change the number of inputs for each person to six MP5 A dd another array/variable to store/total the new option votes Any three from: MP1 Initialise count variables/array for each option MP2 Using a conditional statement to identify preference 1 in at least one option (in the input/stored data) MP3 ... identify preference 1 in all options MP4 ... and adding 1 to the appropriate option count in at least one option MP5 ... for students only MP6 ... using a loop to cycle through the whole array/set of inputs for each option 0478/23 May/June 2021 Section B Three marks for three or four correct Two marks for two correct One mark for one correct Data Type Description Real Strin g Must be a whole numbe r Must be one of two values Ma y be an y numbe r Inte g e r May contain any combination of characters Boolean 0478/23 May/June 2021 One mark per mark point for each piece of test data (3 × two marks) • Correct validation check name • Correct use identified Validation check name Length (check) • Use Counts the number of characters in the data to make sure it isn’t too long (max length 320 characters). 2021 • Validation check name Range (check) • Use Checks that the number entered fits within given parameters Ericson-Bower • Validation check name Type (check) • Use Checks the type of data entered (in this case) to make sure no numbers are present 0478/23 May/June 2021

Correct Algorithm 2 1 Count ← 2 REPEAT 3 FullScore ← 4 INPUT Number StoreLoop ← 5 REPEAT 6 INPUT Score 7 FullScore ← FullScore + Score StoreLoop ← StoreLoop + 1 8 UNTIL StoreLoop = Number 9 OUTPUT "The full score is ", FullScore 10 OUTPUT "Another set of scores (Y or N)?" 11 INPUT Another 12 IF Another = "N" 13 THEN 14 Count ← 15 ENDIF 16 UNTIL Count = 1 0478/23 May/June 2021

One mark per mark point ( Max 4 ) MP1 After line 6 // replace line 6 MP2 ScoreArray[StoreLoop] ← Score // INPUT ScoreArray[StoreLoop] MP3 between lines 8 and 10 MP4 AverageScore ← FullScore/Number MP5 OUTPUT "The average score is ", AverageScore Example correct algorithm for reference from part 4(a) 1 Count ← 2 REPEAT 3 FullScore ← 4 INPUT Number 5 FOR StoreLoop ← 1 TO Number 6 INPUT Score 7 FullScore ← FullScore + Score 8 NEXT 9 OUTPUT "The full score is ", FullScore 10 OUTPUT "Another set of scores (Y or N)?" 11 INPUT Another 12 IF Another = "N" 13 THEN 14 Count ← 15 ENDIF 16 UNTIL Count = 1 0478/23 May/June 2021
